//: Global 'basic' mode catches name resolution, attribute access, and
obvious type mismatches without forcing exhaustive annotations on the
executionEnvironments below opt critical modules into 'strict' so the
scorer library and backtest/attribution math carry full type guarantees.
Why scorers/backtest/attribution: these are the load-bearing surfaces.
Wrong dtype in attribution -> wrong α t-stat -> bad verdict.
Wrong scorer signature -> wrong stock picks. Strict here pays for itself.
Tests excluded: fixture mocks and unittest.MagicMock patterns trip
every name-resolution rule pyright has; we already lint tests with ruff.

executionEnvironments.//: Layer 3 engine — strict so refactors can't silently break BacktestReport contract.
Layer 5 attribution — strict so cost/Carhart/Sharpe inputs are typed end-to-end.
Validated-scorer library — public-API equivalent; strict prevents signature drift.
